Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\ Topology map UI display, linkages, Map Image

Step Action:
Prerequisite: 
Predecessor 67869 has been run

Map Display Filter options- links Info: for Network device to Recon device
On both VMs:
Open Topology page
 Click the filter icon to open Map Display Filters, enable Link Info and close the popup
Navigate to the network switch DEVLAB-HIR-M102
Zoom in enough to see the Inventory Information for Mac Addresses (which is saved in the Perspective editor which should still be selected).
Step Expected:
On both VMs this switch has a link to 2 Recon nodes

 The links have a label at each end (zoom in!) which is the same on both VMs
At the Recon end the label is similar to "Network Adaptor....."
At the switch end the label is a number in the format "x/y" 
See attached screenshot for an example of where to look for this information
NOTE! IN this DB you will see to instances of a VM IAHcVPPASISQA14. This is a mistake in the DB and should be ignored for the purpose of testing

Step Action: Click on the switch to select it
Step Expected: The links are now highlighted in purple when the switch is selected and mouse over the switch

Step Action: Right click the Switch > Details and in the Details, popup select MAC Tab
Step Expected:
The Interface column number for the 2 rows with Type = Endpt match the numbers on the links at the switch end ("x/y") that link to the 2 recon assets 

The 2 rows with the link interface numbers above have the Mac addresses displayed in the Recon Nodes' Perspective information
 The information is the same on both VMs

Step Action: Find the Switch DEVLAB-CIS-2960X
Step Expected:
It has one Recon Device attached.
The link has a label at each end
At the Recon end the label similar to "Network Adaptor....."
At the switch end the label is Gi/1/0/13

Step Action: Right click the Switch > Details and in the Details, popup select MAC Tab
Step Expected:
The Interface column number for the row with Type = Endpt matches the numbers on the link at the switch end that links to the recon node 

The MAC table row with the link interface number above has the Mac address displayed in the Recon Node's Perspective information

Step Action:
Test link Info is correct between Network devices and LLDP Discovery assets
 On both VMs
 In the Details popup for Router DEVLAB-HIR-M102 select LLDP tab.
Step Expected:
Under remote Device column, the linked switches are listed, and the LLDP Discovery Device.
The Local Int numbers match the labels on the links to these switches/LLDP Device
The information is the same on both VMs
[Note: The Network linkages are not correct for all switches. There is an Aha Idea created to readdress how linkages work so until then all we can test is that the display is the same as the previous version]

Step Action: Click on each of the 2 connected switches in turn and open Details - LLDP tab
Step Expected:
The Remote Device column includes the DEVLAB-HIR-M102
The Remote Int is representative of the same link as the Local Int on DEVLAB-HIR-M102, although its in a different format (e.g. port-004-00004 represents 4/4... )
 The information is the same on both VMs

Step Action: Click on the LLDP Discovery asset and bring up Details -Properties
Step Expected: The Remote Data value matches the Local Int on DEVLAB-HIR-M102

Step Action:
Test Map image
 Click the image icon in tool bar
Step Expected:
An image file is created
When opened it shows the Topology map in its current customization: zoom, perspective editor, location

Step Action:
Test delete Perspective selection

Ensure the Perspective you created in the previous test case is selected and to the right of  Select a perspective  drop down click the trash can button
Step Expected: A popup is displayed for you to confirm the deletion

Step Action: Delete the Perspective
Step Expected: The Trashcan button is disabled. The map doesn't change its display

Step Action: click the Select a Perspective drop down
Step Expected: It displays 'NO DATA FOUND'

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\ Topology UI - Asset Details tabs in Prep DB

 \QASTORE01\Database Backups\Integrity Datasets_Databases\REGRESSION TEST DATABASES\Y.Y \Prep Database. Where Y.Y is the released version of integrity

iisreset
 Navigate to Cyber web page - Top level Location is selected

DMZ*.

Step Action: In the navigation popup click the PC
Step Expected: The map highlights the PC you selected. It has either a pink, orange, yellow or light blue background

Step Action: Repeat for each sublocation. When you have tested a sublocation, collapse it again via the '-' button to free up the real estate and select the next location you expect recon devices per step 1

Step Action:
Test Recon Detail Summary tab displays expected data
Expand GASYNB Group Location and DMZ Sublocation and Select a Recon computer node that is pink in color and right click
Step Expected: A dial popup is displayed with one sector of the popup labelled Detail - see example screenshot

Step Action: Select Detail
Step Expected:
A new pop up is displayed defaulting to Summary Tab. See example screenshot
the selected PC name is correct at the top of the popup
The Location string at the top of the popup is correct for the data owner location
The Asset is the Data owner name
Object Type is Computer System
Date Collected matches the year the data was collected  for that Asset
There is hover text available for each field
The Vulnerability counts are displayed at the bottom and there is at least 1 Critical CVE count representing the pink node color

Test export to Excel
 For each Detail tab in previous step click Export to excel
(You will be using these in the following steps)

An excel file is created populated with all the data and columns in the tabs
The data and row count matches between the Excels from the release and build in test

Step Action:
Test Recon details against Asset Hierarchy
 Return to the Details popup Summary tab and note the Manufacturer, IPv4 Address, Model Number and Mac address properties
Return to Asset Hierarchy and expand the tree for the same location, select the data owner and drill down to find the same PC and select it to display Computer Details in the right pane
Step Expected: The Computer Details properties display the same properties as in Topology Summary for Manufacturer, Model, Name and Network Adapter Ip4 and Mac address (found as a Network Adaptor property, probably on page 2 of the grid)

Filter the Recon excel you exported from Topology on Object Type - Processor

Test Recon Inventory tab displays expected data

 Navigate to Inventory > Browse> Computers. Browse by Hardware Locate the recon device from topology and click the # number of inventory items link to display the inventory popup Endpoints components for: <computer name>
In the Search field of the popup paste in the product name you copied
Step Expected: The row count in the Inventory popup match the Topology Inventory xls  for the filtered product.  In Topology:  find a DCS node in any sublocation.

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\ Topology UI\Node association with a Group

Step Action: Prerequisite: Predecessor TC Db connected

Test Node association to an ICS Group within a location
 Reopen or refresh web page if used for predecessor testing (need to do this to clear the cache of location Groups previously opened in Topology)
Go to topology, open the top level Node and expand GASYNB and DMZ Location Groups which have Recon, switches and DCS assets
Right click on any switch or recon node and in the dial popup select Create, wait a second and an Association flyout will appear, click Association (see screenshot)
Step Expected:
There is a popup Associate Node to group and the drop down contains all the DCS nodes in this location only.
(You refreshed the web page because Topology remembers all previously opened Locations even if they are currently collapsed, but you refreshed the webpage and cleared that cache so you only see the ICS Groups in the Location you have just expanded)

Step Action:
Select one of the DCS nodes in the dropdown and click Associate to Group
Wait a few seconds
Step Expected:
The DCS will move across the map and  the switch or recon node will disappear.
 The DCS will be expanded so that its Group boundary background will encompass itself, the DSC, and the associated node, the recon or switch 
If the ICS node doesnt automatically expand, click the '+' and you will see the recon or switch
See screesnhot

Step Action:
Test Creating a new Node

In the currently open Location Group right click in any white space
Step Expected: The dial popup is displayed and Create is enabled, and 2 flyouts are added for Node and Group. Both are enabled if you hover them. See screenshot example

Step Action: Click the flyout for Node
Step Expected: A popup Create node is displayed with fields to populate data. See screenshot

Step Action: Populate all fields, in the Device Type select anything from the list. Click Create Node
Step Expected: The popup closes. (The node will be created somewhere in the real estate box but its a bitch to find so go to next step to verify it got created)

Step Action: Open the Menu icon and enable Navigation menu. Find the Devices of the Device Type you added ( e.g. if you created a Router then look in the router section. See screenshot)
Step Expected: Your new device is listed in the menu

Step Action: Click on the device you created in the navigation menu
Step Expected: You are navigated to that device node on the map and it is highlighted.  Its icon will represent your device selectionThe icon has a cog with a pen in the top corner indicating it is a manually created Node.

Step Action:
Test a new Nodes properties will be displayed in Perspective Editor
Drop down the Perspective Editor Inventory list and check all of the list and Apply
Zoom in on the topology map and navigate to the enlarged new node
Step Expected: All the Properties you populated when creating the node that match an Inventory property are displayed

Step Action:
Test Node association with DCS in a different  Location Group
 Open a different top level Location Group with DCS asset(s). Screenshot the DCS Asset(s) with their names.
Collapse the second location Group.
Now there is a second location cached in Topology, this is the group where you will associate your new node.
Right click on your new Node created in step 6, in the dial popup select Create and select the Associate flyout.
Step Expected: There is a popup dialog Associate Node to Group. The drop down includes all the DCS assets in the second location you expanded and then collapsed

Step Action: Select one of the ICS assets and click Associate to Group
Step Expected: The new node is removed from your current location group

Step Action: Expand the second location group and navigate to the DCS asset you associated your Node with, and expand it
Step Expected: Your node has moved to this asset

Step Action: Refresh the web page and return to Topology and open the location group where you associated your new node, expand the DCS where you made the association
Step Expected: The new node is displayed

Step Action: In the tool bar click the Image icon and open the image png
Step Expected: The image includes the new node

Step Action:
Create a Favorite with a manually associated Node
 Click the save button along side the Favorites field and enter a favorites name
Refresh the webpage. Return to Topology and select your Favorite you just created from the Favorites drop down
Step Expected: The map returns to the opened location and your node is included in the DCS it is associated with

Step Action:
Test remove association of a Node
Go to the DCS with the associated node. Right click on the new node and hover Remove, wait for the Association flyout and click it.
Step Expected: The node is removed from the DCS

Step Action: Expand the original location Group where you created the node
Step Expected: The node is back iin that location group

Step Action:
Test Create a new Group
Refresh the web page and expand the location group from previous step
 In the location, group right click in free white space and in the dial popup select Create and from the flyout select Group
Step Expected: A Create Group popup is displayed

Step Action: Enter a name for the group and create it
Step Expected: The Group is added as a node to the current location

Step Action: Right click on the group (you may have to wait a minute for the web page to enable the popup) In the dial popup hover Create and in the flyout click Node.
Step Expected: This will display the Create Node popup

Step Action:
Populate the name and in Device Type select a different object from the list from the previous node you made, and Create Node
Wait a minute: Ensure the Group is selected (blue outline) and expand it ( the '+' is not immediately visible
Step Expected: The node is in the group with the expected icon representing the device type and the cog with a pencil to indicate its a manually created node

Step Action:
Test Edit Node information
 Right click on the Node within the group and click Edit Node
In the popup change the device type and some other properties and click Edit Node button
Step Expected: The node updates its icon to reflect the different device type

Step Action:
Test move a Node to a group in the same location
Go to the first Node that you created that is now back in the current location (after step 16 above) and right click
Step Expected: The dial popup has enabled the segment for Group, this allows you to move an node into a Group within the current location

Step Action: Click Group
Step Expected: A popup Add Node to Group is displayed with the Group you previously created listed as the only selection

Step Action: Click Move to Group
Step Expected: The Node is moved into the new Group you created

Prerequisite
Predecessor TC 82654, linked,  DB connected

Network Devices
Topology

Select the Baseline for DTL High Risk
Filter Object Name on Contains <Devicename> and note which devices have Addition or Deletion or no deviations. Repeat for each device name
Go to  Topology

Step Action: Expand Perspective Editor for Deviations
Step Expected:
The Baselines drop down shows a total of (X) 
'X' representing the number of Recon Nodes that have Baseline Deviations

Step Action:
Enable Disposition and Age and Apply then collapse the Perspective Editor 
Zoom in on the Map so that the Deviations details are visible for these Recon node(s)
Step Expected: The  Node(s) will show Deviations populated for Unassigned and an Age interval matching the age since the deviation occurred - see screenshot for an example of a Recon node with deviations

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\ Topology map linkages in new database

Step Action:
Prerequisite: New database
  Admin Utility > Asset Models:  Import the  models:
PAS Inventory items
PAS Recon
PAS Cyberintegrity
PAS Network Devices
PAS Topology
 
The datasets are found here: \QASTORE01\zzAutomation_Data\QTP_Data\Integrity Data\PAS Network Devices\ NetworkImport
 QASTORE01\zzAutomation_Data\QTP_Data\Integrity Data\PAS Recon Data\Integrity DTL VMs NEW\Recon DTL VMs ForTopo linkages.7z 
 
Copy them locally and unzip the Recon
Go to Admin Utility > Asset Configurations Add dataowners for Recon and Network devices 
Under Import Folder, Browse to location of your dataset and Save
NOTE! When the recon data is imported, the data gets modified, Always unzip the data from scratch before importing
Go to Schedules - Add schedules for   Recon (Sequence is XML Import All)
 and Network devices (Sequence is All). 
Right click on the Schedules and Run
(if run is not enabled, click the refresh icon at lower left or restart the DataCollector service)
SHARED STEP: Before running latest Asset model PAS Inventory Items schedule:

Running latest Asset model PAS Inventory Items schedule:
Admin Utility: If you have not already done so: Import latest Inventory asset model and create an Asset 
Ensure you have a Cyber Asset model, if not import it and create an asset
Ensure you have Access DB Engine Office 10 64 Bit installed. If not install it from the installer ISS_Setup.exe via Additional Software link in the Installer popup
Go to installation folder \PAS\Integrity\DataCollector\Asset Models\PAS Inventory Items and copy PASInventory.accdb to the \Datacollector folder

Admin Utility > Asset Configurations: Add an Asset for PAS Inventoryitems called Inventory
Under Schedule Add a schedule for Inventory
Select sequence Create Inventory items for PAS Recon without Tree
 Run the schedule
SHARED STEP: Create a Cyber Hierarchy with Inventory

Build a Cyber Hierarchy
Prerequisite: Data owners and  PAS Inventory Items schedule has been run 
Open <Install folder>DataCollector\ConfigurationManager as Admin
 In upper drop down select  CyberInventory.
 Create the top-level location in the Cyber Tree: Right click in left Panel > Add Child Node and name it e.g. Plant A or Enterprise. (there may be an error popup when you do this, click OK and ignore it) 
 
From Asset drop down: Select one of your Assets e.g. Recon 
From Object Type select Data owner from drop down and the data owner name will populate in the panel below. Select the name in the Panel and drag onto the top level node. See screenshot
Right click on the left panel and refresh and the Top Node will have a '+'. 
 Expand '+' and you will see a sublevel node for (example) your Recon or Fox data owner - see second screenshot
 
Repeat for other Recon, Network Devices and ICS assets as applicable.
If you need inventory data perform the next steps. If you dont need inventory data you can stop here

If you have ICS or DCS Assets:
 With your ICS e.g. Fox data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventoryitem
 When selecting ICS assets you will see multiple Inventory items, you want the one with *****DATAOWNER**** in the name 
 Select the ***DATAOWNER*** item and drag it onto the ICS node e.g. the Fox node. See screenshot

If you have Recon Assets:
 With your Recon data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventory_Asset and drag the data owner name (there is only one) from the panel and drop it onto the recon node. See screenshot

If you have Network Devices Assets
 With your Network Devices data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventory_folder and drag NetworkDevices_All  from the panel and drop it onto the Network Devices node.

You dont need to save, it does that automatically
 Post condition:
 Ready for Admin Utility Schedule for Cyber

Admin Utility > Asset Configurations: Add an Asset for PAS Cyber Integrity called Cyber
Under Schedule Add a schedule for Cyber and run it (there is only one sequence)
Create a schedule for Topology and run it (there is only one sequence)
Step Expected: The topology sequence completes without errors

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\ Topology map UI display, Navigation, Perspectives, Favorites

Step Action:
Prerequisite: 
On VM with test build: restore and upgrade the linkages database that is located here:
 \QASTORE01\Database Backups\Integrity Datasets_Databases\REGRESSION TEST DATABASES\7.3.1 \Topology Linkages 
On one of the Release Build VMs (IAHcVPPASSQLQA2 or IAHcVPPASISQA09) restore and connect to a release version of the same database 
Due to a bug fix in 8.0.0.63 - see links- delete the topology asset and re-add in via Admin Utility Asset Configuration and add a new Topology import schedule and run it
SHARED STEP: Applying a Permanent License for Assets and Modules

Open Admin Utility > licensing. Create a new license. 
FOR INTEGRITY. Click Begin and in the popup, click  I agree and click Next till you see the code. Copy the code.
 Go to licensing site ( the link is in Summary because it doesnt navigate to the url from this page)
Paste the code into Installation ID.
Use a QA name - Sue/Nick/Harsha - in Search and click on the name link for a license for PAS Global LLC unless your main TC called for another license )e.g. Georgia-Pacific - Leaf River for GP)
Click Verify 
The assets/modules supported by the license are listed and there is a Temporary license Key displayed below the gridded list. Copy this license and paste it into the admin utility popup in the lower field which says Enter that code here and click Next and Close
This will close Admin Utility and give you a temporary license.
 To get the permanent license: Get the QA you selected in Search to send you the licenseinfo txt file which they will have received in email and drop it into PAS\Integrity folder
Relaunch Admin Utility > licensing

Step Action: SHARED STEP: Create Asset Model Schedule

Create Asset Model Schedule
Datacollector service is running in Admin Utility> Service Administration
 Prerequisite: 
 The Asset model(s) you are going to use has been imported 
 An Asset(s) created in Asset configuration with Browse folder pointing to local dataset
Any Connection databases have been configured in Connection Definitions if applicable 
Go to Admin Utility>Schedules
Click Add
Enter a schedule name that is copied from the Asset Configuration > Name field entry for this dataowner   
Server name will default to your current integrity server
 Go to Sequences Tab. Select your Asset from the drop down
For all Assets apart from recon: select All or something similar like All Sequences from Definitions and click '>' to move it to sequences panel
For Recon scroll down the Definitions list and select All XML
Click OK
The schedule is added. Right click on the schedule row and Run Now
If Run is disabled; restart Data collector service
Step Expected:
There are no errors not previously documented as expected in the main test case
For example: The Recon import will generate expected "errors" - refer to the qastore data folder for a readme.txt

Step Action:
Go to Admin utility > Asset Explorer Configuration and ensure that you see your asset and you can expand the subfolders to see the data objects

SHARED STEP: Create a Cyber Hierarchy with Inventory

Build a Cyber Hierarchy
Prerequisite: Data owners and  PAS Inventory Items schedule has been run 
Open <Install folder>DataCollector\ConfigurationManager as Admin
 In upper drop down select  CyberInventory.
 Create the top-level location in the Cyber Tree: Right click in left Panel > Add Child Node and name it e.g. Plant A or Enterprise. (there may be an error popup when you do this, click OK and ignore it) 
 
From Asset drop down: Select one of your Assets e.g. Recon 
From Object Type select Data owner from drop down and the data owner name will populate in the panel below. Select the name in the Panel and drag onto the top level node. See screenshot
Right click on the left panel and refresh and the Top Node will have a '+'. 
 Expand '+' and you will see a sublevel node for (example) your Recon or Fox data owner - see second screenshot
 
Repeat for other Recon, Network Devices and ICS assets as applicable.
If you need inventory data perform the next steps. If you dont need inventory data you can stop here

If you have ICS or DCS Assets:
 With your ICS e.g. Fox data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventoryitem
 When selecting ICS assets you will see multiple Inventory items, you want the one with *****DATAOWNER**** in the name 
 Select the ***DATAOWNER*** item and drag it onto the ICS node e.g. the Fox node. See screenshot

If you have Recon Assets:
 With your Recon data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventory_Asset and drag the data owner name (there is only one) from the panel and drop it onto the recon node. See screenshot

If you have Network Devices Assets
 With your Network Devices data owner selected in Asset: From Object Type select Inventory_folder and drag NetworkDevices_All  from the panel and drop it onto the Network Devices node.

You dont need to save, it does that automatically
 Post condition:
 Ready for Admin Utility Schedule for Cyber
Return to Admin Utility - Schedules. Add a schedule for Cyber and run it
Go to Asset Explorer Configuration
Step Expected: The assets are created with sub folders for recon and network devices

SHARED STEP: Download and update the latest Vulnerability and/or Patch Management Asset model

Download the latest Vulnerability and/or Patch Management  Asset model from sftp.pas.com
Login with credentials username: PASVulnerabilityMgt, pw Pas!Vul3!21PAS
Select Web option and go to the folder for Vulnerability and/or Patch Management
See screenshot.
Select  the .iam you want (VM Asset model should be a max of 24 hrs old, PM Asset Model should be a max of 1 month old) and Download
Copy Vulnerability.iam/ to 
<Integrity Install>\Integrity\DataCollector\Asset Models\PAS Vulnerability Management and 
Patch Management.iam to <Integrity install>\Integrity\DataCollector\Asset Models\PAS Patch Management
 Reopen Admin utility> Asset Models. You will see the updated model version(s) in the left column.
 
  Import the asset models into the database
Import VM and patch management asset models via Task scheduler -these asset models are far too large to run via Admin utility asset model import. See attached doc for how to do this
NOTE! This usually takes 2-6 hours depending on database size- or longer and may well fail the first time you run it. 
Reopen Admin utility > Asset Models
Step Expected:
Validate no errors in event log
 You will see the latest date of the asset model in the Imported column in Admin Utility>Asset Models

Step Action:
To double check all the data got imported (which isnt guaranteed even if it all looks good so far)
 Go to PAS\Integrity\Data Collector and open Asset Model Builder as Admin
Go to Tools menu and to check VM data click NVD matching and for PM data click Patch Evaluation.
Step Expected:
There is data in both popups, see attachments..it wont be exactly like this but there should be plenty of data
An empty window is Bad and it didnt work

Step Action:
ONLY  For a database that does NOT  contain 4.1 asset model or higher
After importing the VM Asset model the FIRST time
In Admin Utility >Asset Configuration: Ensure there is a VM asset. if not Add an Asset called VM for Asset Model PAS Vulnerability Management.
  In Admin Utility>Schedules create  Schedule for Vulnerability Management - Sequence VM Rectify PropertyNames. 
 Run it. This will take only a minute or so.
Rerun the VM asset model import to add the data into the clean VM database tables
Continue with the steps below

NOTE!! For database upgrades or any TC that requires both Patch Mgt and VM asset model updates , ensure you run the Patch Management schedule BEFORE the VM asset model. The Vmatching uses patch management data and can significantly increase the number of Vmatches

For Patch Management if required for your test case
 In Admin Utility >Asset Configuration: Ensure there is a Patch Management asset. if not Add an Asset called Patch Management for Asset Model PAS Patch Managment
 In the Configuration field Recon Data owners(s)) add ALL the recon dataowners in the database comma delimited like this:
Recon1,Recon2,...etc See screenshot example
Save  
 In Admin Utility>Schedules either create or rerun Schedule for Patch Management - Sequence All
Step Expected: Validate no errors in Schedule import log or event log

To run  Vulnerability matching if required for your test case

This can also take many hours depending on database size
